title: 2026 Wisconsin Governor Election
description: Open 2026 race for governor of Wisconsin, a battleground state where the winner will shape state policy on taxes, schools, health care, abortion, labor, and election administration. With Gov. Tony Evers not seeking reelection, the contest is centered on contrasting Democratic and Republican approaches to affordability, public services, and the state’s response to the Trump administration.

candidates.name: Sara Rodriguez
candidates.summary: Sara Rodriguez is Wisconsin’s lieutenant governor and a former state Assembly member from suburban Milwaukee. She entered the 2026 governor’s race as a Democrat, emphasizing health care, reproductive rights, public schools, and affordability, and has framed her campaign as a response to Donald Trump’s policies.

summary_sources.title: Wisconsin Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez enters 2026 governor race by calling Trump a "maniac"

candidates.name: Tom Tiffany
candidates.summary: Tom Tiffany is a Republican U.S. representative from northern Wisconsin and a candidate for governor in 2026. His campaign has emphasized lowering property taxes, protecting farmland, restoring education standards, and aligning with conservative priorities, while recent reporting has focused on his positions on mail-in voting and other election issues.

summary_sources.title: Governor candidate Tom Tiffany sends mixed signals on mail-in voting

flags.concern: All donor entries for Tom Tiffany are sourced from Tony Evers' OpenSecrets contributor page (2022–2023 cycle), not Tiffany's own fundraising records. The URL references 'officeholders/tony-evers' and the single $90K entry from Wisconsin Democratic Party is implausible for a Republican candidate. This is a critical data error.
flags.suggestion: Replace with Tiffany's actual FEC/OpenSecrets fundraising data. Query OpenSecrets for 'Thomas Tiffany' or use FEC.gov directly for his 2026 gubernatorial committee (not his House committee).

flags.concern: Bill Berrien withdrew from the 2026 race in January 2026 after controversy. Including him as an active candidate is misleading, even though his summary correctly notes the withdrawal. The profile's presence suggests he remains a viable general-election option.
flags.suggestion: Remove Berrien entirely from the candidates array, or clearly mark him as 'withdrawn' with a status field and move him to a separate historical/withdrawn section if the data structure supports it.

flags.concern: Mike Kohn's image URL points to a generic Wisconsin state seal (500px-Seal_of_Wisconsin.svg.png), not an actual photo of the candidate. This is a placeholder.
flags.suggestion: Source or obtain an actual photograph of Mike Kohn, or leave image_url null if unavailable.

flags.concern: David King's image appears to be a photo of the famous British scientist Sir David King (based on the Wikipedia URL structure 'Sir_David_King_(cropped).jpg'), not the Wisconsin gubernatorial candidate David King. This is a mismatched image.
flags.suggestion: Obtain the correct photograph of David King (Wisconsin gubernatorial candidate) or set to null.

flags.concern: Career history array is empty for Mike Kohn, despite him being a candidate. No background information is available in the profile.
flags.suggestion: Research and populate Kohn's professional background, or acknowledge in the summary that detailed biographical information is not publicly available.

flags.concern: Career history array is empty for David King, despite him being a candidate. The summary mentions his campaign themes but not his background.
flags.suggestion: Research and populate King's professional and community background, or acknowledge in the summary that detailed biographical information is limited.

flags.concern: Education array is empty for Mike Kohn.
flags.suggestion: Research and add educational background if publicly available, or acknowledge its absence.

flags.concern: Education array is empty for David King.
flags.suggestion: Research and add educational background if publicly available, or acknowledge its absence.

flags.concern: Both polling entries have null values for sample_size and all matchup percentages. The Marquette and RealClearPolling data provide no actual voter preference information—they appear to be placeholder entries. The URLs reference articles about 'undecided voters' and general polling aggregates, but no actual numbers are populated.
flags.suggestion: Either populate with actual poll numbers from these sources (if publicly available for 2026), or remove the polling array entirely until real data exists. If included, add a note explaining why data is unavailable.

flags.concern: The donor source for Sara Rodriguez references Tony Evers' 2022 OpenSecrets page, not Rodriguez's own fundraising records. While the amounts and organization names may be plausible for a Democratic candidate, the source URL is incorrect (it references Evers, not Rodriguez).
flags.suggestion: Verify and correct the source URL to Rodriguez's own OpenSecrets fundraising page or her FEC filings.

flags.concern: Tom Tiffany's state Senate tenure is listed as 2011–2020, but his Assembly tenure is listed as 2011–2013. These overlap in 2011–2013. Wisconsin state legislators typically serve in one chamber at a time; this sequence needs clarification about whether he served both simultaneously (unlikely) or if dates are wrong.
flags.suggestion: Verify the exact timeline. Tiffany likely served in the Assembly 2011–2013, then moved to the Senate 2013–2020 (not 2011–2020).

flags.concern: The campaign website URL 'https://www.saraforwi.com/' is cited as 'last_accessed': '2026-03-29T00:00:00Z' multiple times throughout the profile. This is extremely recent (profile date is 2026-03-29), which is plausible but worth verifying.
flags.suggestion: Confirm that all campaign websites were actually accessed on 2026-03-29 or update timestamps to reflect actual access dates.

flags.concern: Tom Tiffany's U.S. Representative tenure is marked as ongoing (end_year: null), which is correct as of 2026-03-29. However, if he resigns to run for governor full-time (common in open-seat races), this may become outdated quickly.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a note or monitoring for updates if Tiffany resigns from Congress during the campaign.

flags.concern: The summary correctly notes that Bill Berrien withdrew, but his profile remains in the active candidates array. This creates a structural inconsistency.
flags.suggestion: Remove Berrien from the candidates array as noted above.

flags.concern: The race description mentions 'several independent candidates have limited public visibility.' Three independent candidates (Kohn, King, and presumably others) are included, but Kohn and King have very sparse profiles with mostly 'low confidence' issue positions. This is consistent with the description but highlights a real data gap.
flags.suggestion: No change needed; the description accurately flags the visibility gap. However, consider whether independent candidates warrant inclusion if data is so limited.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates strong sourcing and generally neutral language across the major-party candidates (Rodriguez and Tiffany), with well-documented career histories and issue positions backed by credible news outlets and official sources. However, there are several significant data integrity issues that require correction before publication: (1) a critical donor-data error for Tom Tiffany that pulls from Tony Evers' 2022 donor list instead of Tiffany's own records; (2) inconsistent candidate inclusion—Bill Berrien has withdrawn from the race and should not appear as an active candidate; (3) incomplete and unverified profiles for independent candidates King and Kohn, with placeholder/mismatched images; and (4) empty polling data fields that provide no actual voter preference information. The race description and lead candidate profiles are accurate and well-sourced, but these structural problems must be resolved.

flags.concern: The sources for top donors are incorrectly linked to Tony Evers instead of Sara Rodriguez, leading to potential factual inaccuracies in donor attribution.
flags.suggestion: Update the sources to reflect accurate data for Sara Rodriguez, possibly from OpenSecrets or FEC records specific to her campaign.

flags.concern: Sample size is null, indicating missing data that could affect the completeness and accuracy of polling information.
flags.suggestion: Obtain and include actual sample size data from the pollster to ensure the profile is fully informative.

flags.concern: Percentages are null, making the polling data incomplete and potentially misleading.
flags.suggestion: Replace null values with verified percentages from the source URLs or updated polls.

flags.concern: Bill Berrien has withdrawn from the race, making his profile outdated and potentially irrelevant for the general election focus.
flags.suggestion: Remove or archive this candidate's entry to maintain profile completeness and relevance.

flags.concern: Many issues for Mike Kohn have low confidence and lack detailed stances, indicating gaps in completeness for independent candidates.
flags.suggestion: Gather more detailed information from additional credible sources to enhance the profile's thoroughness.

flags.concern: David King's issues section has several low-confidence stances and missing details, reflecting incompleteness in covering independent candidates.
flags.suggestion: Expand with more specific policy positions from his campaign materials or interviews to improve accuracy and depth.

reviews.summary: The profile is generally well-structured with neutral language and credible sources for major candidates, but it contains factual inaccuracies in donor data and incomplete polling information, which undermine its overall reliability. Specifically, Sara Rodriguez's top donors are sourced from Tony Evers' records, and polling data lacks essential details like sample sizes and percentages.

polling_note: Recent polling shows the race remains largely undefined, with most voters undecided in both party primaries and limited name recognition for the major candidates.
